title: 4.1 DEFINITIONS

data.rule: 4.1 DEFINITIONS

title: The following de fi nitions apply to the provisions of this chapter.

data.rule: The following de fi nitions apply to the provisions of this chapter.

title: FIXED LADDER: A ladder that is permanently attached to a structure, building, or equipment.

data.definition: A ladder that is permanently attached to a structure, building, or equipment.

title: CHAPTER 4

data.rule: CHAPTER 4 LIVE LOADS

title: expected by the intended use or occupancy but shall not be less than the minimum uniformly distributed live loads required by Table 4.3-1.

data.rule: expected by the intended use or occupancy but shall not be less than the minimum uniformly distributed live loads required by Table 4.3-1.

title: GRAB BAR SYSTEM: Abar and associated anchorages and attachments to the structural system, for the support of body weight in locations such as toilets, showers, and tub enclosures.

data.definition: Abar and associated anchorages and attachments to the structural system, for the support of body weight in locations such as toilets, showers, and tub enclosures.

title: GUARD SYSTEM: A component or assembly of components, including anchorages and attachments to the structural system, near open sides of an elevated surface for the purpose of minimizing the possibility

data.definition: A component or assembly of components, including anchorages and attachments to the structural system, near open sides of an elevated surface for the purpose of minimizing the possibility of a fall from the elevated surface by people, equipment, or material.

title: HANDRAIL SYSTEM: A rail grasped by hand for guidance and support and associated anchorages and attachments to the structural system.

data.definition: A rail grasped by hand for guidance and support and associated anchorages and attachments to the structural system.

title: HELIPAD: A structural surface that is used for landing, taking off, taxiing, and parking helicopters.

data.definition: A structural surface that is used for landing, taking off, taxiing, and parking helicopters.

title: LIVE LOAD: A load produced by the use and occupancy of the building or other structure that does not include construction or environmental loads, such as wind load, snow load, rain load, earthquake lo

data.definition: A load produced by the use and occupancy of the building or other structure that does not include construction or environmental loads, such as wind load, snow load, rain load, earthquake load, fl ood load, or dead load.

title: ROOF LIVE LOAD: A load on a roof produced (1) during maintenance by workers, equipment, and materials, and (2) during the life of the structure by movable objects, such as planters or other similar sm

data.definition: A load on a roof produced (1) during maintenance by workers, equipment, and materials, and (2) during the life of the structure by movable objects, such as planters or other similar small decorative appurtenances that are not occupancy related. An occupancy-related live load on a roof such as rooftop assembly areas, rooftop decks, and vegetative or landscaped roofs with occupiable areas, is considered to be a live load rather than a roof live load.

title: SCREEN ENCLOSURE: A building or part thereof, in whole or in part self-supporting, having walls and a roof of insect or sun screening using fi berglass, aluminum, plastic, or similar lightweight netti

data.definition: A building or part thereof, in whole or in part self-supporting, having walls and a roof of insect or sun screening using fi berglass, aluminum, plastic, or similar lightweight netting material, which encloses an occupancy or use such as outdoor swimming pools, patios or decks, and horticultural and agricultural production facilities.

title: VEHICLE BARRIER SYSTEM: A system of components, including anchorages and attachments to the structural system near open sides or walls of garage fl oors or ramps, that acts as a restraint for vehicles

data.definition: A system of components, including anchorages and attachments to the structural system near open sides or walls of garage fl oors or ramps, that acts as a restraint for vehicles.

title: 4.2 LOADS NOT SPECIFIED

data.rule: 4.2 LOADS NOT SPECIFIED

title: For occupancies or uses not designated in this chapter, the live load shall be determined in accordance with a method approved by the Authority Having Jurisdiction.

data.rule: For occupancies or uses not designated in this chapter, the live load shall be determined in accordance with a method approved by the Authority Having Jurisdiction.

title: 4.3 UNIFORMLY DISTRIBUTED LIVE LOADS

data.rule: 4.3 UNIFORMLY DISTRIBUTED LIVE LOADS

title: 4.3.1 Required Live Loads

data.rule: 4.3.1 Required Live Loads

title: The live loads used in the design of buildings and other structures shall be the maximum loads

data.rule: The live loads used in the design of buildings and other structures shall be the maximum loads

title: 4.3.2 Provision for Partitions In of fi ce buildings and in other buildings where partition locations are subject to change, provisions for partition weight shall be made, whether or not partitions ar

data.rule: 4.3.2 Provision for Partitions In of fi ce buildings and in other buildings where partition locations are subject to change, provisions for partition weight shall be made, whether or not partitions are shown on the plans. The partition load shall not be less than 15 psf (0.72 kN/m 2 ) and shall not be reduced per Section 4.7.

title: EXCEPTION: A partition live load is not required where the minimum speci fi ed live load is 80 psf (3.83 kN/m 2 ) or greater.

data.rule: EXCEPTION: A partition live load is not required where the minimum speci fi ed live load is 80 psf (3.83 kN/m 2 ) or greater.

title: 4.3.3 Partial Loading

data.rule: 4.3.3 Partial Loading

title: The uniform live load applied only to a portion of a structure or member shall be accounted for if it produces a more unfavorable load effect than the uniform live load applied over the full structure

data.rule: The uniform live load applied only to a portion of a structure or member shall be accounted for if it produces a more unfavorable load effect than the uniform live load applied over the full structure or member. Uniform live loads applied to a portion of a structure or member are permitted to be reduced in accordance with Section 4.7.

title: 4.3.3.1 Partial Loading of Roofs

data.rule: 4.3.3.1 Partial Loading of Roofs

title: Where uniform roof live loads are reduced to less than 20 psf (0.96 kN/m 2 ) in accordance with Section 4.8.2 and are applied to the design of structural members arranged so as to create continuity, t

data.rule: Where uniform roof live loads are reduced to less than 20 psf (0.96 kN/m 2 ) in accordance with Section 4.8.2 and are applied to the design of structural members arranged so as to create continuity, the reduced roof live load shall be applied to adjacent spans or to alternate spans, whichever produces the greatest unfavorable load effect.

title: 4.3.4 Interior

data.rule: 4.3.4 Interior

title: Walls and Partitions Interior walls and partitions that exceed 6 ft (1.83 m) in height, including their fi nish materials, shall have adequate strength and stiffness to resist the loads to which they

data.rule: Walls and Partitions Interior walls and partitions that exceed 6 ft (1.83 m) in height, including their fi nish materials, shall have adequate strength and stiffness to resist the loads to which they are subjected but not less than a horizontal load of 5 psf (0.240 kN/m 2 ).

title: 4.4 CONCENTRATED LIVE LOADS

data.rule: 4.4 CONCENTRATED LIVE LOADS

title: Floors, roofs, and other similar surfaces shall be designed to support the uniformly distributed live loads prescribed in Section 4.3 or the concentrated live loads given in Table 4.3-1, whichever pro

data.rule: Floors, roofs, and other similar surfaces shall be designed to support the uniformly distributed live loads prescribed in Section 4.3 or the concentrated live loads given in Table 4.3-1, whichever produces the greater load effects. Unless otherwise speci fi ed, the indicated concentrated load shall be assumed to be uniformly distributed over an area of 2.5 ft (762 mm) by 2.5 ft (762 mm) and shall be located so as to produce the maximum load effects in the members.

title: 4.5 LOADS ON HANDRAIL, GUARD, GRAB BAR, AND VEHICLE BARRIER SYSTEMS, AND ON SHOWER SEATS AND FIXED LADDERS

data.rule: 4.5 LOADS ON HANDRAIL, GUARD, GRAB BAR, AND VEHICLE BARRIER SYSTEMS, AND ON SHOWER SEATS AND FIXED LADDERS

title: 4.5.1 Handrail and Guard Systems

data.rule: 4.5.1 Handrail and Guard Systems

title: Handrail and guard systems shall be designed to resist a single concentrated load of 200 lb (0.89 kN) applied in any direction at any point on the

data.rule: Handrail and guard systems shall be designed to resist a single concentrated load of 200 lb (0.89 kN) applied in any direction at any point on the

title: handrail or top rail to produce the maximum load effect on the element being considered and to transfer this load through the supports to the structure.

data.rule: handrail or top rail to produce the maximum load effect on the element being considered and to transfer this load through the supports to the structure.

title: 4.5.1.1 Uniform Load

data.rule: 4.5.1.1 Uniform Load

title: Handrail and guard systems shall also be designed to resist a load of 50 lb ∕ ft (pound-force per linear foot) (0.73 kN/m) applied in any direction along the handrail or top rail and to transfer this

data.rule: Handrail and guard systems shall also be designed to resist a load of 50 lb ∕ ft (pound-force per linear foot) (0.73 kN/m) applied in any direction along the handrail or top rail and to transfer this load through the supports to the structure. This load need not be assumed to act concurrently with the concentrated load speci fi ed in Section 4.5.1.

title: EXCEPTIONS: The uniform load need not be considered for the following occupancies:

data.rule: EXCEPTIONS: The uniform load need not be considered for the following occupancies: Oneand two-family dwellings;

title: Factory, industrial, and storage occupancies in areas that are not accessible to the public and that serve an occupant load not greater than 50; and

data.rule: Factory, industrial, and storage occupancies in areas that are not accessible to the public and that serve an occupant load not greater than 50; and

title: Roofs not intended for occupancy.

data.rule: Roofs not intended for occupancy.

title: 4.5.1.2 Guard System Component Loads Balusters, panel fi llers, and guard system in fi ll components, including all rails except the handrail and the top rail, shall be designed to resist a horizontal

data.rule: 4.5.1.2 Guard System Component Loads Balusters, panel fi llers, and guard system in fi ll components, including all rails except the handrail and the top rail, shall be designed to resist a horizontally applied normal load of 50 lb (0.22 kN) on an area not to exceed 12 in. × 12 in. (305 mm × 305 mm), including openings and space between rails and located so as to produce the maximum load effects. Reactions due to this loading are not required to be superimposed with the loads speci fi ed in Sections 4.5.1 and 4.5.1.1.

title: 4.5.2 Grab Bar Systems and Shower Seats

data.rule: 4.5.2 Grab Bar Systems and Shower Seats

title: Grab bar systems and shower seats shall be designed to resist a single concentrated load of 250 lb (1.11 kN) applied in any direction at any point on the grab bar or shower seat to produce the maximum

data.rule: Grab bar systems and shower seats shall be designed to resist a single concentrated load of 250 lb (1.11 kN) applied in any direction at any point on the grab bar or shower seat to produce the maximum load effect.

title: 4.5.3 Vehicle Barrier Systems

data.rule: 4.5.3 Vehicle Barrier Systems

title: Vehicle barrier systems for passenger vehicles shall be designed to resist a single

data.rule: Vehicle barrier systems for passenger vehicles shall be designed to resist a single

title: Equation (4.7-1)

data.expression: Vehicle barrier systems in garages L=L 0.25þpffiffi 1 ffiffiffi 5 ffiffiffiffiffiffiffiffi (4.7-1)

L.description: Inside length of a rectangular tank, parallel to the direction of the earthquake force being investigated;

title: concentrated load of 6,000 lb (26.70 kN) applied horizontally in any direction to the barrier system and shall have anchorages or attachments capable of transferring this load to the structure. For de

data.rule: concentrated load of 6,000 lb (26.70 kN) applied horizontally in any direction to the barrier system and shall have anchorages or attachments capable of transferring this load to the structure. For design of the system, the load shall be assumed to act at heights between 1 ft 6 in. (460 mm) and 2 ft 3 in. (686 mm) above the fl oor or ramp surface, located to produce the maximum load effects. The load shall be applied on an area not to exceed 12 in. × 12 in. (305 mm × 305 mm). This load is not required to act concurrently with any handrail or guard system loadings speci fi ed in Section 4.5.1. Vehicle barrier systems in garages accommodating trucks and buses shall be designed in accordance with AASHTO LRFD Bridge Design Specifications .

title: 4.5.4 Fixed Ladders Fixed ladders with rungs shall be designed to resist a single concentrated load of 300 lb (1.33 kN) applied at any point to produce the maximum load effect on the element being con

data.rule: 4.5.4 Fixed Ladders Fixed ladders with rungs shall be designed to resist a single concentrated load of 300 lb (1.33 kN) applied at any point to produce the maximum load effect on the element being considered. The number and position of additional concentrated live load units shall be a minimum of 1 unit of 300 lb (1.33 kN) for every 10 ft (3.05 m) of ladder height.

title: Where rails of fi xed ladders extend above a fl oor or platform at the top of the ladder, each side rail extension shall be designed to resist a single concentrated live load of 100 lb (0.445 kN) appl

data.rule: Where rails of fi xed ladders extend above a fl oor or platform at the top of the ladder, each side rail extension shall be designed to resist a single concentrated live load of 100 lb (0.445 kN) applied in any direction at any height up to the top of the side rail extension. Ships ladders with treads instead of rungs shall be designed to resist the stair loads given in Table 4.3-1.

title: 4.6 IMPACT LOADS

data.rule: 4.6 IMPACT LOADS

title: 4.6.1 General

data.rule: 4.6.1 General

title: The live loads speci fi ed in Sections 4.3 through 4.5 shall be assumed to include adequate allowance for ordinary impact conditions. Provision shall be made in the structural design for uses and load

data.rule: The live loads speci fi ed in Sections 4.3 through 4.5 shall be assumed to include adequate allowance for ordinary impact conditions. Provision shall be made in the structural design for uses and loads that involve unusual vibration and impact forces.

title: 4.6.2 Elevators All elements subject to dynamic loads from elevators shall be designed for impact loads and de fl ection limits prescribed by ASME A17.1.

data.rule: 4.6.2 Elevators All elements subject to dynamic loads from elevators shall be designed for impact loads and de fl ection limits prescribed by ASME A17.1.

title: 4.6.3 Machinery For the purpose of design, the weight of machinery and moving loads shall be increased as follows to allow for impact: (1) light machinery, shaftor motor-driven, 20%; and (2) reciproca

data.rule: 4.6.3 Machinery For the purpose of design, the weight of machinery and moving loads shall be increased as follows to allow for impact: (1) light machinery, shaftor motor-driven, 20%; and (2) reciprocating machinery or power-driven units, 50%. All percentages shall be increased where speci fi ed by the manufacturer.

title: 4.6.4 Elements Supporting Hoists for Façade Access and

data.rule: 4.6.4 Elements Supporting Hoists for Façade Access and

title: Building Maintenance Equipment Structural elements that support hoists for façade access and building maintenance equipment shall be designed for a live load of 2.5 times the rated load of the hoist o

data.rule: Building Maintenance Equipment Structural elements that support hoists for façade access and building maintenance equipment shall be designed for a live load of 2.5 times the rated load of the hoist or the stall load of the hoist, whichever is larger.

title: 4.6.5 Fall

data.rule: 4.6.5 Fall

title: Arrest, Lifeline, and Rope Descent System Anchorages Fall arrest, lifeline, and rope descent system anchorages and the structural elements that support these anchorages shall be designed for a live lo

data.rule: Arrest, Lifeline, and Rope Descent System Anchorages Fall arrest, lifeline, and rope descent system anchorages and the structural elements that support these anchorages shall be designed for a live load of 3,100 lb (13.8 kN) for each attached line in any direction that the load may be applied.

title: Anchorages of horizontal lifelines and the structural elements that support the anchorages shall be designed for the maximum tension that develops in the horizontal lifeline from these live loads.

data.rule: Anchorages of horizontal lifelines and the structural elements that support the anchorages shall be designed for the maximum tension that develops in the horizontal lifeline from these live loads.

title: 4.7 REDUCTION IN UNIFORM LIVE LOADS

data.rule: 4.7 REDUCTION IN UNIFORM LIVE LOADS

title: 4.7.1 General

data.rule: 4.7.1 General

title: Except for roof uniform live loads, all other minimum uniformly distributed live loads, Lo , in Table 4.3-1, are permitted to be reduced in accordance with the requirements of Sections 4.7.2 through 4

data.rule: Except for roof uniform live loads, all other minimum uniformly distributed live loads, Lo , in Table 4.3-1, are permitted to be reduced in accordance with the requirements of Sections 4.7.2 through 4.7.6.

title: 4.7.2 Reduction in Uniform Live Loads

data.rule: 4.7.2 Reduction in Uniform Live Loads

title: Subject to the limitations of Sections 4.7.3 through 4.7.6, members for which the value of KLLAT is 400 ft 2 (37.16 m 2 ) or more are permitted to be designed for a reduced uniform live load in accord

data.rule: Subject to the limitations of Sections 4.7.3 through 4.7.6, members for which the value of KLLAT is 400 ft 2 (37.16 m 2 ) or more are permitted to be designed for a reduced uniform live load in accordance with the following formula: where

title: L = Reduced design live load per ft 2 (m 2 ) of area supported by the member;

data.rule: L = Reduced design live load per ft 2 (m 2 ) of area supported by the member;

title: Lo = Unreduced design live load per ft 2 (m 2 ) of area supported by the member (see Table 4.3-1);

data.rule: Lo = Unreduced design live load per ft 2 (m 2 ) of area supported by the member (see Table 4.3-1);

title: KLL = Live load element factor (see Table 4.7-1); and

data.rule: KLL = Live load element factor (see Table 4.7-1); and

title: AT = Tributary area in ft 2 (m 2 ).

data.rule: AT = Tributary area in ft 2 (m 2 ).

title: L shall not be less than 0.50 Lo for members supporting one fl oor, and L shall not be less than 0.40 Lo for members supporting two or more fl oors.

data.rule: L shall not be less than 0.50 Lo for members supporting one fl oor, and L shall not be less than 0.40 Lo for members supporting two or more fl oors.

title: 4.7.3 Heavy Live Loads

data.rule: 4.7.3 Heavy Live Loads

title: Live loads that exceed 100 lb/ft 2 (4.79 kN/m 2 ) shall not be reduced.

data.rule: Live loads that exceed 100 lb/ft 2 (4.79 kN/m 2 ) shall not be reduced.

title: EXCEPTION: Live loads for members supporting two or more fl oors are permitted to be reduced by a maximum of 20%, but the reduced live load shall not be less than L as calculated in Section 4.7.2.

data.rule: EXCEPTION: Live loads for members supporting two or more fl oors are permitted to be reduced by a maximum of 20%, but the reduced live load shall not be less than L as calculated in Section 4.7.2.

title: 4.7.4 Passenger Vehicle Garages

data.rule: 4.7.4 Passenger Vehicle Garages

title: The live loads shall not be reduced in passenger vehicle garages.

data.rule: The live loads shall not be reduced in passenger vehicle garages.

title: EXCEPTION: Live loads for members supporting two or more fl oors are permitted to be reduced by a maximum of 20%, but the reduced live load shall not be less than L as calculated in Section 4.7.2.

data.definition: Live loads for members supporting two or more fl oors are permitted to be reduced by a maximum of 20%, but the reduced live load shall not be less than L as calculated in Section 4.7.2.

title: 4.7.5 Assembly Area Loads

data.rule: 4.7.5 Assembly Area Loads

title: Live loads shall not be reduced in assembly areas.

data.rule: Live loads shall not be reduced in assembly areas.

title: Table 4.7-1. Live Load Element Factor, KLL .

data.rule: Table 4.7-1. Live Load Element Factor, KLL .

title: * In lieu of the preceding values, KLL is permitted to be calculated.

data.rule: * In lieu of the preceding values, KLL is permitted to be calculated.

title: 4.7.6 Limitations on One-Way Slabs

data.rule: 4.7.6 Limitations on One-Way Slabs

title: The tributary area, AT , for one-way slabs shall not exceed an area de fi ned by the slab span times a width normal to the span of 1.5 times the slab span.

data.rule: The tributary area, AT , for one-way slabs shall not exceed an area de fi ned by the slab span times a width normal to the span of 1.5 times the slab span.

title: 4.8 REDUCTION IN UNIFORM ROOF LIVE LOADS

data.rule: 4.8 REDUCTION IN UNIFORM ROOF LIVE LOADS

title: 4.8.1 General

data.rule: 4.8.1 General

title: The minimum uniformly distributed roof live loads, Lo , in Table 4.3-1, are permitted to be reduced in accordance with the requirements of Sections 4.8.2 and 4.8.3.

data.rule: The minimum uniformly distributed roof live loads, Lo , in Table 4.3-1, are permitted to be reduced in accordance with the requirements of Sections 4.8.2 and 4.8.3.

title: 4.8.2 Ordinary Roofs, Awnings, and Canopies

data.rule: 4.8.2 Ordinary Roofs, Awnings, and Canopies

title: Ordinary fl at, pitched, and curved roofs, and awning and canopies other than those of fabric construction supported by a skeleton structure, are permitted to be designed for a reduced uniform roof li

data.rule: Ordinary fl at, pitched, and curved roofs, and awning and canopies other than those of fabric construction supported by a skeleton structure, are permitted to be designed for a reduced uniform roof live load, as speci fi ed in Equation (4.8-1), or other controlling combinations of loads, as speci fi ed in Chapter 2, whichever produces the greater load effect. In structures such as greenhouses, where special scaffolding is used as a work surface for workers and materials during maintenance and repair operations, a lower roof load than speci fi ed in Equation (4.8-1) shall not be used unless approved by the Authority Having Jurisdiction. On such structures, the minimum roof live load shall be 12 psf (0.58 kn/m 2 ).

title: where Lr is the reduced roof live load per ft 2 (m 2 ) of horizontal projection supported by the member, and Lo is the unreduced design roof live load per ft 2 (m 2 ) of horizontal projection supporte

data.rule: where Lr is the reduced roof live load per ft 2 (m 2 ) of horizontal projection supported by the member, and Lo is the unreduced design roof live load per ft 2 (m 2 ) of horizontal projection supported by the member (see Table 4.3-1).

title: The reduction factors R 1 and R 2 shall be determined as follows:

data.rule: The reduction factors R 1 and R 2 shall be determined as follows: in SI:

title: where AT is the tributary area in ft 2 (m 2 ) supported by the member and

data.rule: where AT is the tributary area in ft 2 (m 2 ) supported by the member and

title: where, for a pitched roof, F is the number of inches of rise per foot (in SI: F = 0.12 × slope, with slope expressed in percentage points) and, for an arch or dome, F is the rise-to-span ratio multipl

data.rule: where, for a pitched roof, F is the number of inches of rise per foot (in SI: F = 0.12 × slope, with slope expressed in percentage points) and, for an arch or dome, F is the rise-to-span ratio multiplied by 32.

title: 4.8.3 Occupiable Roofs Roofs that have an occupancy function, such as roof gardens or other special purposes, are permitted to have their uniformly distributed live load reduced in accordance with the

data.rule: 4.8.3 Occupiable Roofs Roofs that have an occupancy function, such as roof gardens or other special purposes, are permitted to have their uniformly distributed live load reduced in accordance with the requirements of Section 4.7.

title: Roofs used for other special purposes shall be designed for appropriate loads as approved by the Authority Having Jurisdiction.

data.rule: Roofs used for other special purposes shall be designed for appropriate loads as approved by the Authority Having Jurisdiction.

title: 4.9 CRANE LOADS

data.rule: 4.9 CRANE LOADS

title: 4.9.1 General

data.rule: 4.9.1 General

title: The crane live load shall be the rated capacity of the crane. Design loads for the runway beams, including connections and support brackets, of moving bridge cranes and monorail cranes shall include t

data.rule: The crane live load shall be the rated capacity of the crane. Design loads for the runway beams, including connections and support brackets, of moving bridge cranes and monorail cranes shall include the maximum wheel loads of the crane and the vertical impact, lateral, and longitudinal forces induced by the moving crane.

title: 4.9.2 Maximum Wheel Load

data.rule: 4.9.2 Maximum Wheel Load

title: The maximum wheel loads shall be the wheel loads produced by the weight of the bridge, as applicable, plus the sum of the rated capacity and the weight of the trolley with the trolley positioned on it

data.rule: The maximum wheel loads shall be the wheel loads produced by the weight of the bridge, as applicable, plus the sum of the rated capacity and the weight of the trolley with the trolley positioned on its runway at the location where the resulting load effect is maximized.

title: 4.9.3 Vertical Impact Force

data.rule: 4.9.3 Vertical Impact Force

title: The maximum wheel loads of the crane determined in accordance with Section 4.9.2 shall be increased by the percentages shown in the following text to account for the effects of vertical impact or vibr

data.rule: The maximum wheel loads of the crane determined in accordance with Section 4.9.2 shall be increased by the percentages shown in the following text to account for the effects of vertical impact or vibration: Monorail cranes (powered) 25%

title: Bridge crane service class D, E, or F 25%

data.rule: Bridge crane service class D, E, or F 25%

title: Bridge crane service class A, B, or C 10%

data.rule: Bridge crane service class A, B, or C 10%

title: Cranes with hand-geared bridge, trolley, and hoist

data.rule: Cranes with hand-geared bridge, trolley, and hoist 0%

title: 4.9.3.1 Bridge Crane Service Class For the purpose of determining the vertical impact force, one of the following bridge crane service classes shall be assigned based on the actual service conditions,

data.rule: 4.9.3.1 Bridge Crane Service Class For the purpose of determining the vertical impact force, one of the following bridge crane service classes shall be assigned based on the actual service conditions, including the frequency of use, variability in load lifted, and the operation speed.

title: Bridge Crane Service Class A (Standby or infrequent service): This service class shall include cranes used in installations such as powerhouses, public utilities, turbine rooms, motor rooms, and trans

data.rule: Bridge Crane Service Class A (Standby or infrequent service): This service class shall include cranes used in installations such as powerhouses, public utilities, turbine rooms, motor rooms, and transformer stations where precise handling of equipment at slow speeds is required and cranes are infrequently used or are idled for long periods. Full rated loads shall be handled for initial installation of equipment and for infrequent maintenance.

title: Bridge Crane Service Class B (Light service): This service class shall include cranes used in repair shops, light assembly operations, service buildings, light warehousing, etc. where service requirem

data.rule: Bridge Crane Service Class B (Light service): This service class shall include cranes used in repair shops, light assembly operations, service buildings, light warehousing, etc. where service requirements are light and the speed is slow. Loads are permitted to vary, but full rated loads shall occur only occasionally, with two to fi ve lifts per hour, averaging 10 ft (3.05 m) per lift.

title: Bridge Crane Service Class C (Moderate service): This service class shall include cranes used in machine shops or paper mill machine rooms, etc. where service requirements are moderate. In this type o

data.rule: Bridge Crane Service Class C (Moderate service): This service class shall include cranes used in machine shops or paper mill machine rooms, etc. where service requirements are moderate. In this type of service, the crane shall handle loads which average 50% of the rated capacity with fi ve to ten lifts per hour, averaging 15 ft (4.6 m), not over 50% of the lifts at rated capacity.

title: Bridge Crane Service Class D (Heavy service): This service class shall include cranes used in heavy machine shops, foundries, fabricating plants, steel warehouses, container yards, lumber mills, etc.,

data.rule: Bridge Crane Service Class D (Heavy service): This service class shall include cranes used in heavy machine shops, foundries, fabricating plants, steel warehouses, container yards, lumber mills, etc., and the standard duty bucket and magnet operations where heavy duty production is required. In this type of service, the crane shall handle loads approaching 50% of the rated capacity constantly during the working period. High speeds are used for this type of service with 10 to 20 lifts per hour averaging 15 ft (4.6 m), not over 65% of the lifts at rated capacity.

title: Bridge Crane Service Class E (Severe service): This service class shall include cranes capable of handling loads approaching rated capacity throughout its life. Applications include magnet, bucket, ma

data.rule: Bridge Crane Service Class E (Severe service): This service class shall include cranes capable of handling loads approaching rated capacity throughout its life. Applications include magnet, bucket, magnet/bucket combination cranes for scrap yards, cement mills, lumber mills, fertilizer plants, container handling, etc., with 20 or more lifts per hour at or near the rated capacity.

title: Equation (4.8-1)

data.expression: roofs, and awning and canopies other than those of fabric construction supported by a skeleton structure, are permitted to be designed for a reduced uniform roof live load, as speci fi ed in Equation (4.8-1)

a.description: 10% of least horizontal dimension or 0.4 h ,whichever is smaller, but not less than either 4% of least horizontal dimension or 3 ft (0.9 m)

title: Bridge Crane Service Class F (Continuous severe service): This service class shall include cranes capable of handling loads approaching rated capacity continuously under severe service conditions thro

data.rule: Bridge Crane Service Class F (Continuous severe service): This service class shall include cranes capable of handling loads approaching rated capacity continuously under severe service conditions throughout its life. Applications include custom designed specialty cranes essential to performing the critical work tasks affecting the total production facility. These cranes shall provide the highest reliability with special attention to ease of maintenance features.

title: 4.9.4 Lateral Force

data.rule: 4.9.4 Lateral Force

title: The lateral force on crane runway beams with electrically powered trolleys shall be calculated as 20% of the sum of the rated capacity of the crane and the weight of the hoist and trolley. The lateral

data.rule: The lateral force on crane runway beams with electrically powered trolleys shall be calculated as 20% of the sum of the rated capacity of the crane and the weight of the hoist and trolley. The lateral force shall be assumed to act horizontally at the traction surface of a runway beam, in either direction perpendicular to the beam, and shall be distributed with due regard to the lateral stiffness of the runway beam and supporting structure.

title: 4.9.5 Longitudinal Force

data.rule: 4.9.5 Longitudinal Force

title: The longitudinal force on crane runway beams, except for bridge cranes with hand-geared bridges, shall be calculated as 10% of the maximum wheel loads of the crane. The longitudinal force shall be ass

data.rule: The longitudinal force on crane runway beams, except for bridge cranes with hand-geared bridges, shall be calculated as 10% of the maximum wheel loads of the crane. The longitudinal force shall be assumed to act horizontally at the traction surface of a runway beam in either direction parallel to the beam.

title: GARAGE AND VEHICULAR FLOOR LOADS

data.rule: GARAGE AND VEHICULAR FLOOR LOADS

title: 4.10.1 Passenger Vehicle Garages

data.rule: 4.10.1 Passenger Vehicle Garages

title: Floors in garages and portions of a building used for the storage of motor vehicles shall be designed for the uniformly distributed live loads of Table 4.3-1 or the following concentrated load: (1) fo

data.rule: Floors in garages and portions of a building used for the storage of motor vehicles shall be designed for the uniformly distributed live loads of Table 4.3-1 or the following concentrated load: (1) for garages restricted to passenger vehicles accommodating not more than nine passengers, 3,000 lb (13.35 kN) acting on an area of 4.5 in. × 4.5 in. (114 mm × 114 mm); and (2) for mechanical parking structures without slab or deck that are used for storing passenger vehicles only 2,250 lb (10 kN) per wheel.

title: 4.10.2 Truck and Bus Garages

data.rule: 4.10.2 Truck and Bus Garages

title: Live loads in garages and portions of a building used for the storage of trucks and buses shall be in accordance with the AASHTO LRFD Bridge Design Specifications ; however, provisions for fatigue and

data.rule: Live loads in garages and portions of a building used for the storage of trucks and buses shall be in accordance with the AASHTO LRFD Bridge Design Specifications ; however, provisions for fatigue and dynamic load allowance therein are not required to be applied.

title: EXCEPTION: The vehicular live loads and load placement in garages and portions of a building used for the storage of trucks or buses are permitted to be determined using the actual vehicle weights for

data.rule: EXCEPTION: The vehicular live loads and load placement in garages and portions of a building used for the storage of trucks or buses are permitted to be determined using the actual vehicle weights for the vehicles allowed onto the garage fl oors, provided that such loads and placement are based on rational engineering principles and are approved by the Authority Having Jurisdiction. The load effects from the actual vehicle weights shall not be less than the effects from a uniform live load of 50 psf (2.40 kN/m 2 ). This live load shall not be reduced.

title: 4.10.3 Sidewalks, Vehicular Driveways, and Yards Subject

data.rule: 4.10.3 Sidewalks, Vehicular Driveways, and Yards Subject

title: to Trucking Uniform loads for sidewalks, vehicular driveways, and yards subject to truck loads, other than that provided in Table 4.3-1, shall also be considered where appropriate, in accordance with

data.rule: to Trucking Uniform loads for sidewalks, vehicular driveways, and yards subject to truck loads, other than that provided in Table 4.3-1, shall also be considered where appropriate, in accordance with an approved method which contains provisions for truck loads. The concentrated wheel load provided in Table 4.3-1 shall be applied on an area of 4.5 in. × 4.5 in. (114 mm × 114 mm).

title: 4.10.4 Emergency

data.rule: 4.10.4 Emergency

title: Vehicle Loads Where a structure or portions of a structure are accessed by fi re department vehicles and other emergency vehicles, those portions of the structure subjected to such loads shall be desi

data.rule: Vehicle Loads Where a structure or portions of a structure are accessed by fi re department vehicles and other emergency vehicles, those portions of the structure subjected to such loads shall be designed for one of the following:

title: (a) The actual operational loads, including the total vehicle load, the individual wheel loads, and the outrigger reactions, whichever produces the greater load effects, as stipulated and approved by

data.rule: (a) The actual operational loads, including the total vehicle load, the individual wheel loads, and the outrigger reactions, whichever produces the greater load effects, as stipulated and approved by the Authority Having Jurisdiction. The loads shall include the combined weight of the vehicle and equipment. The total vehicle load, individual wheel loads, and outrigger reactions do not act concurrently.

title: (b) The design truck and design tandem live loads in the AASHTO LRFD Bridge Design Specifications . The design truck and the design tandem need not be assumed to act concurrently. The AASHTO provision

data.rule: (b) The design truck and design tandem live loads in the AASHTO LRFD Bridge Design Specifications . The design truck and the design tandem need not be assumed to act concurrently. The AASHTO provisions for fatigue and dynamic load allowance are not required to be applied.

title: Emergency vehicle loads need not be assumed to act concurrently with other uniform live loads.

data.rule: Emergency vehicle loads need not be assumed to act concurrently with other uniform live loads.

title: EXCEPTION: Emergency vehicle loads need not be considered for portions of the structure where physical barriers such as bollards, vehicle barriers, or overhead clearance bars restrict emergency vehicl

data.rule: EXCEPTION: Emergency vehicle loads need not be considered for portions of the structure where physical barriers such as bollards, vehicle barriers, or overhead clearance bars restrict emergency vehicle access.
4.11 HELIPAD LOADS

title: 4.11.1 General The live loads shall not be reduced. The labeling of helicopter capacity shall be as required by the Authority Having Jurisdiction.

data.rule: 4.11.1 General The live loads shall not be reduced. The labeling of helicopter capacity shall be as required by the Authority Having Jurisdiction.

title: 4.11.2 Concentrated

data.rule: 4.11.2 Concentrated

title: Helicopter Loads Two single concentrated loads, 8 ft (2.44 m) apart, shall be applied on the landing area (representing the helicopter ' s two main landing gear, whether skid type or wheeled type), ea

data.rule: Helicopter Loads Two single concentrated loads, 8 ft (2.44 m) apart, shall be applied on the landing area (representing the helicopter ' s two main landing gear, whether skid type or wheeled type), each having a magnitude of 0.75 times the maximum takeoff weight of the helicopter and located to produce the maximum load effect on the structural elements under consideration. The concentrated loads shall be applied over an area of 8 in. × 8 in. (200 mm × 200 mm) and are not required to act concurrently with other uniform or concentrated live loads.

title: A single concentrated load of 3,000 lb (13.35 kN) shall be applied over an area of 4.5 in. × 4.5 in. (114 mm × 114 mm), located so as to produce the maximum load effects on the structural elements und

data.rule: A single concentrated load of 3,000 lb (13.35 kN) shall be applied over an area of 4.5 in. × 4.5 in. (114 mm × 114 mm), located so as to produce the maximum load effects on the structural elements under consideration. The concentrated load is not required to act concurrently with other uniform or concentrated live loads.

title: 4.12 UNINHABITABLE ATTICS

data.rule: 4.12 UNINHABITABLE ATTICS

title: 4.12.1 Uninhabitable Attics without Storage

data.rule: 4.12.1 Uninhabitable Attics without Storage

title: In residential occupancies, uninhabitable attic areas without storage are those where the maximum clear height between the joist and rafter is less than 42 in. (1,067 mm) or where there are not two or

data.rule: In residential occupancies, uninhabitable attic areas without storage are those where the maximum clear height between the joist and rafter is less than 42 in. (1,067 mm) or where there are not two or more adjacent trusses with web con fi gurations capable of accommodating an assumed rectangle 42 in. (1,067 mm) in height by 24 in. (610 mm) in width, or greater, within the plane of the trusses. The live load in Table 4.3-1 need not be assumed to act concurrently with any other live load requirement.

title: 4.12.2 Uninhabitable

data.rule: 4.12.2 Uninhabitable

title: Attics with Storage In residential occupancies, uninhabitable attic areas with storage are those where the maximum clear height between the joist and rafter is 42 in. (1,067 mm) or greater or where th

data.rule: Attics with Storage In residential occupancies, uninhabitable attic areas with storage are those where the maximum clear height between the joist and rafter is 42 in. (1,067 mm) or greater or where there are two or more adjacent trusses with web con fi gurations capable of accommodating an assumed rectangle 42 in. (1,067 mm) in height by 24 in. (610 mm) in width, or greater, within the

title: plane of the trusses. For attics constructed of trusses, the live load in Table 4.3-1 need only be applied to those portions of the bottom chords where both of the following conditions are met:

data.rule: plane of the trusses. For attics constructed of trusses, the live load in Table 4.3-1 need only be applied to those portions of the bottom chords where both of the following conditions are met:

title: The attic area is accessible from an opening not less than 20 in. (508 mm) in width by 30 in. (762 mm) in length that is located where the clear height in the attic is a minimum of 30 in. (762 mm); an

data.rule: The attic area is accessible from an opening not less than 20 in. (508 mm) in width by 30 in. (762 mm) in length that is located where the clear height in the attic is a minimum of 30 in. (762 mm); and

title: The slope of the truss bottom chord is no greater than 2 units vertical to 12 units horizontal (9.5% slope).

data.rule: The slope of the truss bottom chord is no greater than 2 units vertical to 12 units horizontal (9.5% slope).

title: The remaining portions of the bottom chords shall be designed for a uniformly distributed concurrent live load of not less than 10 lb/ft 2 (0.48 kN/m 2 ).

data.rule: The remaining portions of the bottom chords shall be designed for a uniformly distributed concurrent live load of not less than 10 lb/ft 2 (0.48 kN/m 2 ).
4.13 LIBRARY STACK ROOMS

title: The live loading for library stack rooms provided in Table 4.3-1 applies to stack room fl oors that support nonmobile, doublefaced library book stacks subject to the following limitations:

data.rule: The live loading for library stack rooms provided in Table 4.3-1 applies to stack room fl oors that support nonmobile, doublefaced library book stacks subject to the following limitations:

title: The nominal book stack unit height shall not exceed 90 in. (2,290 mm);

data.rule: The nominal book stack unit height shall not exceed 90 in. (2,290 mm);

title: The nominal shelf depth shall not exceed 12 in. (305 mm) for each face;

data.rule: The nominal shelf depth shall not exceed 12 in. (305 mm) for each face;

title: Parallel rows of double-faced book stacks shall be separated by aisles not less than 36 in. (914 mm) wide.

data.rule: Parallel rows of double-faced book stacks shall be separated by aisles not less than 36 in. (914 mm) wide.
4.14 SEATING FOR ASSEMBLY USES

title: In addition to the vertical live loads provided in Table 4.3-1 for bleachers, folding and telescopic seating, and grandstands, and for stadiums and arenas with fi xed seats fastened to the fl oor, the

data.rule: In addition to the vertical live loads provided in Table 4.3-1 for bleachers, folding and telescopic seating, and grandstands, and for stadiums and arenas with fi xed seats fastened to the fl oor, the design shall include horizontal swaying forces applied to each row of the seats as follows:

title: 24 lb per linear ft (0.35 kN/m) of seat applied in a direction parallel to each row of seats; and

data.rule: 24 lb per linear ft (0.35 kN/m) of seat applied in a direction parallel to each row of seats; and

title: 10 lb per linear ft (0.15 kM/m) of seat applied in a direction perpendicular to each row of seats.

data.rule: 10 lb per linear ft (0.15 kM/m) of seat applied in a direction perpendicular to each row of seats.

title: The parallel and perpendicular horizontal swaying forces need not be applied simultaneously.

data.rule: The parallel and perpendicular horizontal swaying forces need not be applied simultaneously.
4.15 STAIR TREADS

title: The concentrated load on stair treads provided in Table 4.3-1 for stair and exit ways, and for stairs in oneand two-family dwellings, shall be applied on an area of 2 in. × 2 in. (50 mm × 50 mm) and i

data.rule: The concentrated load on stair treads provided in Table 4.3-1 for stair and exit ways, and for stairs in oneand two-family dwellings, shall be applied on an area of 2 in. × 2 in. (50 mm × 50 mm) and is to be applied nonconcurrently with the uniform load.

title: 4.16 SOLAR PANEL LOADS

data.rule: 4.16 SOLAR PANEL LOADS

title: 4.16.1 Roof Loads at Solar Panels

data.rule: 4.16.1 Roof Loads at Solar Panels

title: Roof structures that support solar panel systems shall be designed to resist each of the following conditions:

data.rule: Roof structures that support solar panel systems shall be designed to resist each of the following conditions:

title: The uniform and concentrated roof live loads speci fi ed in Table 4.3-1 with the solar panel system dead loads.

data.rule: The uniform and concentrated roof live loads speci fi ed in Table 4.3-1 with the solar panel system dead loads.

title: EXCEPTION: The roof live load need not be applied to the area covered by solar panels where the clear space between the panels and the roof surface is 24 in. (610 mm) or less.

data.rule: EXCEPTION: The roof live load need not be applied to the area covered by solar panels where the clear space between the panels and the roof surface is 24 in. (610 mm) or less.

title: The uniform and concentrated roof live loads speci fi ed in Table 4.3-1 without the solar panel system present.

data.rule: The uniform and concentrated roof live loads speci fi ed in Table 4.3-1 without the solar panel system present.

title: 4.16.2 Load Combination Roof systems that provide support for solar panel systems shall be designed for the load combinations speci fi ed in Chapter 2.

data.rule: 4.16.2 Load Combination Roof systems that provide support for solar panel systems shall be designed for the load combinations speci fi ed in Chapter 2.

title: 4.16.3 Open-Grid

data.rule: 4.16.3 Open-Grid

title: Roof Structures Supporting Solar Panels Structures with open-grid framing and no roof deck or sheathing supporting solar panel systems shall be designed to support the uniform and concentrated roof li

data.rule: Roof Structures Supporting Solar Panels Structures with open-grid framing and no roof deck or sheathing supporting solar panel systems shall be designed to support the uniform and concentrated roof live loads speci fi ed in Table 4.3-1, except that the uniform roof live load shall be permitted to be reduced to 12 psf (0.57 kN/m 2 ).

title: CONSENSUS STANDARDS AND OTHER REFERENCED DOCUMENTS

data.rule: CONSENSUS STANDARDS AND OTHER REFERENCED DOCUMENTS

title: This section lists the consensus standards and other documents that shall be considered part of this standard to the extent referenced in this chapter.

data.rule: This section lists the consensus standards and other documents that shall be considered part of this standard to the extent referenced in this chapter.

title: AASHTO LRFD Bridge Design Specifications , 7th ed., American Association of State Highway and Transportation Of fi cials, 2014, with 2015 interim revisions.

data.rule: AASHTO LRFD Bridge Design Specifications , 7th ed., American Association of State Highway and Transportation Of fi cials, 2014, with 2015 interim revisions.

title: Cited in: Sections 4.5.3, 4.10.2, and 4.10.4

data.rule: Cited in: Sections 4.5.3, 4.10.2, and 4.10.4

title: ASME A17.1, Safety Code for Elevators and Escalators , American Society of Mechanical Engineers, 2016.

data.rule: ASME A17.1, Safety Code for Elevators and Escalators , American Society of Mechanical Engineers, 2016. Cited in: Section 4.6.2
